author: @iamkian
text: Is it not called an hanG drum? Also known as Hang pan?

replies.author: @NorthernKitty
replies.text: It's similar, but the hang drum doesn't have tongues.  It was actually inspired by the hang drum, however, and was originally made from propane tanks.  Thus the name:  hang + tank = hank.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering

author: @jasonwitt8619
text: What if you sit it up on a piece of rubber feet or actually make a base so that you can bolt it to the center under it. Then it should have a clearer sound because it would have a even vibration. Was just a thought I had in mind due to the fact I am an old drummer and took 4 years of drums back when I was doing the whole school thing.

replies.author: @jasonwitt8619
replies.text: A center rod welded to it would work real nice and then bolt that rod to a flat piece of lumber board under it. That would do real well for improving the sound when hit with a rubber mallet.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: nice tips mate - thanks for taking the time to share

replies.author: @jasonwitt8619
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering I also looked up a few pro's and how they build it and they seem to be all cutting out a 6-8 inch wide hole in the bottom to give the hollow sound and they sit it on little marble size rubber feet so that their is a short distance for the hollow bounce of bass sounds. They sound wonderful

author: @allhumansarejusthuman.5776
text:
Very nice sound to it! 
I would recommend avoiding JB weld though just use fasteners 

In my machine shop we frequently use J-B Weld to dampen tool vibrations on long overhanging lathe tools 😅
Something about the vibrations trying to go through different material qualities really seems to dampen it a bit more than you would expect

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: nice tip mate cheers
